name: check_access_tier
description: Determine the access tier for an incoming message sender. Returns integer tier: 0=stranger (unauthorized), 1=chat-only (basic conversation only), 2=trusted (public tools), 3=owner (full access). Use this as the first step on every incoming message before any processing. If tier is 3, proceed with normal full-access response. If tier is 0, use handle_stranger. For tiers 1-2, apply enforce_tier restrictions before responding.

senderId.description: Sender's unique identifier: phone number with country code (e.g., +15551234567), Telegram numeric user ID, or Discord numeric user ID. Must be normalized before storage.

platform.description: Messaging platform the message originated from. Used for ID normalization and notification routing.

name: handle_stranger
description: Handle an incoming message from an unauthorized contact (tier 0). Executes the full stranger flow: sends the configured diplomatic deflection message to the stranger, notifies the owner via the configured notifyChannel with the first 100 characters of the stranger's message, stores the contact in pendingApprovals, and returns NO_REPLY. Use this when check_access_tier returns tier 0. This is the primary protection mechanism - it deflects unknown contacts while alerting the owner for approval.

senderId.description: Stranger's unique identifier: phone number, Telegram ID, or Discord ID (must be normalized before storage)

platform.description: Messaging platform the stranger message came from

message.description: The stranger's full incoming message. Will be truncated to first 100 characters for the owner notification.

name: approve_contact
description: Process the owner's reply to a stranger notification. Parses the owner's response and updates access-control.json: 'approve'/'yes'/'trusted' grants tier 2 (trusted), 'chat'/'chat-only'/'chat only' grants tier 1 (chat-only), 'block'/'no'/'deny' adds to blockedIds, 'ignore' removes from pendingApprovals without action. Use this when the owner responds to a notification sent by handle_stranger. After approval, sends a notification message to the newly approved contact.

ownerResponse.description: Owner's response text. Valid values: 'approve', 'yes', or 'trusted' (grants tier 2); 'chat', 'chat-only', or 'chat only' (grants tier 1); 'block', 'no', or 'deny' (blocks contact); 'ignore' (removes from pending without action). Case-insensitive.

senderId.description: The contact's sender ID to approve, block, or ignore. Must match the ID stored in pendingApprovals.

name: enforce_tier
description: Enforce access restrictions based on the sender's tier before responding to any non-owner message. Tier 1 (chat-only): respond conversationally only, do NOT use any tools (read, write, exec, web_search), do NOT access memory files, do NOT mention owner's name. Tier 2 (trusted): conversational responses plus public tools (web_search, weather, time/date), do NOT use read/write/exec tools, do NOT share private info (calendar, emails, files). If requested action exceeds tier permissions, return a polite limitation message instead. Do NOT use this for tier 0 - use handle_stranger instead.

tier.description: Access tier to enforce. 1 = chat-only (conversation only, no tools), 2 = trusted (conversation plus public tools only). Do not pass tier 0 or 3 here - handle those with handle_stranger or normal response flow.

requestedAction.description: The action the user is attempting: e.g., 'read file', 'send message', 'search web', 'get weather'. Used to check if the action is permitted at the given tier.

name: normalize_id
description: Normalize a sender ID for consistent matching and storage. Phone numbers (WhatsApp, Signal): strip all non-digit characters except leading '+', then ensure country code is present (e.g., +1 555 123 4567 becomes +15551234567). Telegram: prefix with 'telegram:' followed by numeric user ID. Discord: prefix with 'discord:' followed by numeric user ID. Use this before any access-control.json lookups or storage.

rawId.description: Raw sender ID as received from the platform API. For phones: may include spaces, dashes, parentheses. For Telegram/Discord: may be numeric string.

platform.description: Messaging platform. Required to determine normalization rules (phone-based vs numeric ID-based).

name: check_rate_limit
description: Check if the sender has exceeded their per-tier message rate limit before responding. Rate limits: tier 0 (stranger): 1 message/hour, 3/day; tier 1 (chat-only): 20/hour, 100/day; tier 2 (trusted): 50/hour, 500/day; tier 3 (owner): unlimited. Use this before every response. If limit exceeded, return the deflection message 'I've reached my chat limit for now. Try again later!' instead of the normal response. Updates hourCount/dayCount and reset timestamps in access-control.json.

senderId.description: Sender's normalized ID (must match the ID stored in rate limit tracking). Use normalize_id first if you have a raw ID.

tier.description: Access tier: 0=stranger, 1=chat-only, 2=trusted, 3=owner. Determines which rate limit bucket applies.

name: log_event
description: Log an access control event to the audit log (memory/access-control-log.json). Events include: 'deflected' (stranger message deflected), 'approved' (contact approved by owner), 'blocked' (contact blocked by owner), 'ignored' (owner chose to ignore), 'rate_limited' (sender hit rate limit). Maintains last 100 entries, dropping older ones. Use this after every access control action for compliance and debugging.

action.description: The action type to log. 'deflected' for stranger deflections, 'approved' for successful approvals, 'blocked' for blocks, 'ignored' for owner ignores, 'rate_limited' for rate limit hits.

platform.description: Messaging platform the event occurred on

message.description: First 50 characters of the message (required for 'deflected' events, optional for others)

name: init_access_control
description: Initialize the access-control.json configuration file in the agent's memory directory. Creates the file with default structure: empty ownerIds, empty approvedContacts/pendingApprovals/blockedIds, default strangerMessage template, and rateLimits object. Run this once during skill setup. After initialization, manually edit the file to add ownerIds (owner phone numbers, Telegram IDs, or Discord IDs), set notifyChannel ('telegram', 'whatsapp', 'discord', or 'signal'), set notifyTarget (owner's ID on that channel), and customize strangerMessage with the agent's name.

memoryDir.description: Directory path where to create access-control.json. Default is 'memory' in the workspace root. Do not include trailing slash.

agentName.description: Agent name to substitute in the default strangerMessage template ({{AGENT_NAME}} placeholder). Customize the strangerMessage after initialization for full customization.
